entry.title: SAPUI5 Custom App: Shipment Tracking with SAPUI5 and Google Maps Integration
entry.content:
Business Case:

In the logistics industry, real-time shipment tracking is a necessity. Traditional systems often lack a visual component, leaving businesses without critical insights into routes, distances, and potential delays. Shipment Tracking Application, built with SAPUI5 and Google Maps, addresses these challenges by combining route visualization, detailed instructions, and multilingual support into a single application.

Features:

1. Interactive Map Integration

  • Seamless integration with Google Maps to display real-time shipment routes.
  • Dynamic updates based on user input, such as shipment ID.

2. Step-by-Step Directions

  • Instructions and distances for each step in the route.
  • Multilingual support to cater to global users.

3. Shipment Search

  • Simple search functionality to retrieve shipment routes using shipment IDs.

4. Real-Time Distance Calculation

  • Display total distance for the shipment route instantly.

Setup Instructions

Step 1: Configure Google Maps API
To integrate Google Maps, you need to set up an API key:

  1. Go to the Google Developers Console.
  2. Create or select a project:
    • If you don’t have an existing project, create a new one.
  3. Enable Google Maps JavaScript API:
    • Search for "Google Maps JavaScript API" in the library and enable it.
  4. Create API Credentials:
    • Navigate to the "Credentials" page.
    • Generate a Browser Key for your application.
  5. Set API Restrictions:
    • Restrict the key to your application’s domain for security purposes.

Example API URL for integration:

 

 

 

https://maps.googleapis.com/maps/api/js?key=YOUR_API_KEY&callback=initMap

 

Replace YOUR_API_KEY with the key generated in the Google Developers Console. 

Step 2: Configure OData Service

  • Create an OData service with an entity set, e.g., ShipmentSet.
  • Ensure the service fetches StartCoords and EndCoords based on the shipment number.

 

{
    "StartCoords": { "lat": 30.0444, "lng": 31.2357 },
    "EndCoords": { "lat": 31.2023, "lng": 29.9014 }
}

 

 

XML View

 

 

<mvc:View controllerName="shiptrack.controller.View1"
    xmlns:mvc="sap.ui.core.mvc" displayBlock="true 
    xmlns:core="sap.ui.core"
    xmlns="sap.m" xmlns:l="sap.ui.layout">
    <Page id="page" title="Shipment Tracker">
        <VBox>
            <SearchField id="searchField" placeholder="Enter Shipment ID" search=".onFindShipment" />
            <Text id="totalDistanceText" text="Total Distance: {route>/distance}" />
            <l:Splitter orientation="Horizontal" width="100%" height="500px">
                <l:Splitter>
                    <!-- Directions List -->
                    <List id="directionsList" items="{route>/steps}">
                        <CustomListItem>
                            <VBox>
                                <Text text="{route>instruction}" />
                                <Text text="{route>distance}" />
                            </VBox>
                        </CustomListItem>
                    </List>
                </l:Splitter>
                <l:Splitter>
                    <!-- Map Container -->
                     <Core:HTML id="mapContainer" width="70%" height="500px" />
                </l:Splitter>
            </l:Splitter>
        </VBox>
    </Page>
</mvc:View>

 

 

 

 

Full Controller Code:

 

 

sap.ui.define([
    "sap/ui/core/mvc/Controller",
    "sap/m/MessageToast",
    "sap/ui/model/json/JSONModel"
], function (Controller, MessageToast, JSONModel) {
    "use strict";

    return Controller.extend("shiptrack.controller.View1", {
        onInit: function () {
            this._map = null;
            this._directionsService = null;
            this._directionsRenderer = null;

            const oModel = new JSONModel({ steps: [], distance: "" });
            this.getView().setModel(oModel, "route");

            this._loadGoogleMaps().then(() => {
                this._initializeMap();
            }).catch((error) => {
                console.error("Google Maps could not be loaded:", error);
                MessageToast.show("Unable to load Google Maps. Please try again later.");
            });
        },

        _loadGoogleMaps: function () {
            return new Promise((resolve, reject) => {
                if (typeof google !== "undefined" && google.maps) {
                    resolve();
                    return;
                }
                const script = document.createElement("script");
                script.src="https://maps.googleapis.com/maps/api/js?key=YOUR_API_KEY&libraries=geometry";
                script.async = true;
                script.defer = true;
                script.onload = resolve;
                script.onerror = reject;
                document.head.appendChild(script);
            });
        },

        _initializeMap: function () {
            const mapContainer = this.byId("mapContainer").getDomRef();
            this._map = new google.maps.Map(mapContainer, {
                center: { lat: 0, lng: 0 },
                zoom: 2
            });
            this._directionsService = new google.maps.DirectionsService();
            this._directionsRenderer = new google.maps.DirectionsRenderer({ map: this._map });
        },

        onFindShipment: function () {
            const shipmentId = this.byId("searchField").getValue();
            if (!shipmentId) {
                MessageToast.show("Please enter a shipment ID.");
                return;  }

   // Fetch shipment details using OData service.
         /*   const oDataModel = this.getOwnerComponent().getModel(); // 
              Replace with your OData model.
            oDataModel.read(`/ShipmentSet('${shipmentNumber}')`, {
                success: (data) => {
                    const startCoords = data.StartCoords;
                    const endCoords = data.EndCoords;
                    this._displayRoute(startCoords, endCoords);
                },
                error: (error) => {
                    console.error("Failed to fetch shipment details:", 
                     error);
                    MessageToast.show("Unable to retrieve shipment 
                 details.");
                }
            }); */


            const startCoords = { lat: 30.0444, lng: 31.2357 };
            const endCoords = { lat: 31.2023, lng: 29.9014 };

            this._displayRoute(startCoords, endCoords);
        },

        _displayRoute: function (startCoords, endCoords) {
            const request = {
                origin: startCoords,
                destination: endCoords,
                travelMode: google.maps.TravelMode.DRIVING
            };

            const oModel = this.getView().getModel("route");

            this._directionsService.route(request, (result, status) => {
                if (status === google.maps.DirectionsStatus.OK) {
                    this._directionsRenderer.setDirections(result);

                    const route = result.routes[0];
                    const distance = route.legs[0].distance.text;
                    const steps = route.legs[0].steps.map((step) => ({
                        instruction: step.instructions.replace(/<[^>]+>/g, ''),
                        distance: step.distance.text
                    }));

                    oModel.setProperty("/steps", steps);
                    oModel.setProperty("/distance", distance);

                    MessageToast.show(`Total distance: ${distance}`);
                } else {
                    MessageToast.show("Unable to retrieve directions.");
                }
            });
        }
    });
});

 

 

 

 

Final Demo Video

Watch the application in action: 


Conclusion

With the combination of SAPUI5 and Google Maps API, our Shipment Tracking Application delivers a powerful solution for real-time logistics management. By following the provided setup instructions and integrating the Google Maps API securely, you can create an efficient and user-friendly tool for your business. 

Feel free to implement this application in your SAP projects and customize it as per your needs.
Happy coding! 😉

entry.title: Re: SAP Business AI Cheat Sheet – Your Quick Reference Guide
entry.content:
Excelente documento que va hacer muy útil a la hora de seguir un paso a paso de todo lo que envuelve IA.

Gracias por compartir Raja Gupta.

entry.title: Living SAP Joint Venture Accounting (JVA) - Part 2
entry.content:
After demonstrating how to record the agreement between the operator and non-operator parties, we will now illustrate how to document the ventures to which the Joint Operation Agreement ratios and other JOA information will be applied.

By doing so, we will address our fourth question:

4. How to record or extend agreement activities?

Continuing with our imagination, as friends who decided to start a business together and have already agreed on the terms and share percentages in the Joint Operating Agreement (JOA).

We are now ready to launch either one venture ( Project ) or more ventures (Projects) that are controlled under our outlined agreement . Each venture will consist of phases, known as Equity Types and under each equity type, we will link the operator and the share percentages for each partner, based on what we agreed upon in our JOA.

In such a case the venture is agreement under the joint operating agreement in which two or more parties agree to work together for a common purpose.

Remembering the initial concepts of JVA where : 

 1. We are going to be partners in case that operational activities are capital intensive, involve high risk, and possibly long payback periods.

2. This enables us to spread the risk by sharing in operational costs and revenues in proportion to our investments.

In order to conduct the risk sharing of operation costs we are going to  determine it by the date when this costs accrued and this can be known based costs document date in SAP. 

Then we should determine each document is related to each venture and related to which document date to determine exactly this document happens under each phase ( Equity type ) and each ( Equity group).

Equity types can be actual or logical activity which is linking venture and JOA equity groups in Oil and Gas industry we commonly classified Equity groups as: 

  • Research and Development ( R&D)

Exploration 

Development

  • Engineering and design

Pre-FEED (Preliminary Front-End Engineering Design)

Front End Engineering Design (FEED)

  • Construction, and production

Production Oil

Production Gas

  • Decommissioning

However, Any project or project phase that would be funded and executed only by our company this should be considered a corporate effort and neither the costs or revenues related to this phase are going to be shared.

Let's have a look on sample Equity group that should be maintained on venture master data under transaction code GJVV:

As highlighted, equity groups will link financial transactions to the allocation percentages of partner shares based on the transaction date. However, to connect financial transactions, such as expenses or revenues, to a specific venture, we need to extract the venture data from the cost objects assigned to each transaction.

SAP cost objects like:

  • Cost centers

  • Internal orders

  • Projects and work breakdown structures (WBS elements)

  • Profit centers

Can have the venture data either required or optional based on JVA cost object setup.

SAP JVA is integrated across all cost objects in the system. This is important for linking financial documents with the relevant venture information, whether in the Financial module or other modules like HCM, MM, Project Systems, etc.

Once a posting done, JVA captures ALL financial and cost postings in real time from standard modules and converts them to JV format.

However, at this stage, the financial document may contain a lot of JVA data, but it doesn’t provide the information needed to determine whether the document should be split between partners or if it is not part of this sharing agreement. 

In general, documents that are part of the sharing agreement have a billing nature, meaning the cost is to be divided between partners, and at the same time, each partner's portion must be formally communicated to them.

The costs to be divided between partners ( shared costs ) are called recoverable or Cutback related costs as after the operator pay for it, he should recover, cutback or claim those costs back from non-operator partners.

The costs that should be communicated formally ( legally Invoiced ) to partners are part of billing process and called related to billing.

The term billable expenses in business generally represent the recoverable and relating to billing expense and vice versa for non-billable. 

The billing indicator in JVA is assigned to each posting line in the financial documents. Based on this, the system determines whether the transaction should be recovered and whether it should be included in the billing process.

The financial documents line items can inherit the recovery indicator at the following level of postings:

1. Document type 

2. GL Account master data

3. Cost object 

4. Manual override during posting ( In the financial document )

5. JVA integration manager

Each venture can have a different set of posting rules that control the GL accounts used for transactions related to this venture, such as cash requests from partners (cash calls) or cost recovery processes (cutback of expenses). These posting rules are part of the venture master data.

The funding process for a venture can be managed by a funding group, which determines the bank data (accounts, house bank, and bank ID) to be used for this venture or a group of ventures."

at this stage we can now determine for each financial document either it should be recovered, be part of billing process , related to which venture ( based on the cost object ) and what is the recovery ratios ( based on the equity type and date stored in the venture master data).

Summary:

We’ve already covered how to record the agreement between operator and non-operator parties in a Joint Operation Agreement (JOA). Now, we focus on documenting the ventures to which JOA ratios and other relevant details will apply.

For each venture, we define phases (Equity Types) where partners' share percentages are set according to the JOA. These ventures, controlled under the JOA, help manage joint operational risks, and cost-sharing is based on the transaction date in SAP.

In SAP, financial transactions are linked to ventures through cost objects (like cost centers, internal orders, and projects). These cost objects capture the venture data needed for proper cost allocation.

The system determines if costs should be recovered and / or billed based on recovery indicators, and based on the posting rules set in the venture master data it determine how the related JVA document to be post.

Finally, the funding process for ventures is managed by a funding group, which controls the bank details for each venture.

In the next blog, we should continue answering common partnership questions that JVA can cover.

entry.title: SAP GUI for Java’s 25th Anniversary Edition brings back multi-colored icons
entry.content:
In December 1999, the first public beta version of SAP GUI for Java has been published, followed by the general availability in June 2000 announced at TechEd in Las Vegas.
At that time, the platforms were Mac OS 8, OS/2, HP-UX, Solaris, Linux, AIX and Windows 95/98/NT/00) and the theme was EnjoySAP.

SAP GUI for Java 4.6D with EnjoySAP theme

25 years later, SAP GUI for Java 8.10 rev 1 is available and can be considered an anniversary edition. It brings back the multi-colored icons that have been missed by many since Belize. 

Icons in Signature Design, Corbu, Blue Crystal, Belize, Quartz, Horizon and "Horizon+"

When using Horizon themes they can be activated in the Preferences dialog on the Themes panel with the checkbox "Use multi-colored icons".

Activate multi-colored icons in preferencesABAP Workbench with multi-colored icons in Evening HorizonA list of more new features and bug fixes in SAP GUI for Java 8.10 rev 1 is available in SAP note 3485691.

entry.title: 🌟 Foi incrível! Obrigado por fazer parte do SAP Inside Track Curitiba 2024! 🌟
entry.content:
Há um mês, nos reunimos para um dia repleto de aprendizado, trocas de experiências e conexões valiosas. O SAP Inside Track Curitiba 2024 superou todas as expectativas, e tudo isso graças a vocês: palestrantes, participantes, patrocinadores e organizadores!

✨Destaques do evento:

Palestras inspiradoras com especialistas que trouxeram as últimas tendências do universo SAP.

Discussões enriquecedoras sobre inovações e desafios do mercado.

Networking que abriu portas para novas parcerias e projetos.

Cases reais de empresas que estão liderando a transformação digital com SAP.

💬E você, o que achou?
Queremos ouvir o seu feedback para que os próximos eventos sejam ainda melhores! 

📆Até o próximo encontro!
Já estamos ansiosos para o próximo SAP Inside Track Curitiba. Fique de olho nas nossas redes para novidades e outros eventos da comunidade.

Mais uma vez, obrigado por fazer parte desta jornada incrível. A comunidade SAP só cresce com a sua participação!

#SITCuritiba #SAPInsideTrack #Gratidão #ComunidadeSAP #Networking

entry.title: Http_Communication_Failure in IF_HTTP_CLIENT-->receive
entry.content:
Hi experts,
Im consumin an api in my abap code. but it resulting in dump "http communicaiton failure". while i debugged the program i found it gives dump at receive methode call. need steps to resolve it asap, code is attached. thanks in advance.
TRY.
data(lv_url) = |http://******|.  " here is my url
data: o_client type ref to if_http_client.
cl_http_client=>CREATE_BY_URL( EXPORTING
                               URL = lv_url
                               IMPORTING
                                 client = o_client
                                 EXCEPTIONS
                                   ARGUMENT_NOT_FOUND = 1
                                   PLUGIN_NOT_ACTIVE = 2
                                   INTERNAL_ERROR = 3 ).
if sy-subrc <> 0.
  o_client->CLOSE( ).
  endif.
if o_client is bound.
  o_client->REQUEST->SET_METHOD( if_http_request=>CO_REQUEST_METHOD_POST ).
  o_client->REQUEST->SET_CONTENT_TYPE( 'application/json' ).

*  Set Item Details - Ended
    /UI2/CL_JSON=>SERIALIZE(
    EXPORTING
    DATA = IT_DATA        " Data to serialize
    PRETTY_NAME = /UI2/CL_JSON=>PRETTY_MODE-CAMEL_CASE
    RECEIVING
    R_JSON = GV_JSON ).   " JSON string

    o_client->REQUEST->SET_CDATA( GV_JSON ).

    o_client->SEND( timeout = if_http_client=>co_timeout_default ).
    o_client->RECEIVE( ).

    data: lv_http_Status type i,
          lv_status_text type string.

    o_client->RESPONSE->GET_STATUS(
                                   IMPORTING
                                     code = lv_http_status
                                     reason = lv_status_text ).

    write: lv_status_text.



  endif.

CATCH cx_root into data(lv_text) .

ENDTRY.

Edrilan Berisha

entry.title: Re: SAP GUI for Java’s 25th Anniversary Edition brings back multi-colored icons
entry.content:
Dear RMW,
what great news for Christmas and 2025. Our users will really appreciate it, they have missed it a lot, a small topic with a huge impact. Only the best for the next 25 years.
Best wishes, Merry Christmas and a happy New Year 2025
Henning

entry.title: The settlement rule receiver : Issue with S/4 HANA EAM WOs
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

Requirement: The settlement rule receiver should be selected based on the Account Assignment maintained in the Technical Object. For example, if a Cost Center (CTR) is maintained, then upon releasing the Work Order (WO), the system should designate the Cost Center as the receiver. Similarly, if a Work Breakdown Structure (WBS) is maintained, then upon releasing the WO, the system should designate the WBS as the receiver.

Changes Which I have performed in SPRO as In Settlement profile

  1. Valid receivers: CC: Settlement Optional and WBS Element: Settlement Optional
  2. Default Object Type in Default Values: Kept as Blank ( I tried this with CTR as well but no luck)

While trying to release WO one with CTR maintained in account Assignment of Technical Object and one with WBS maintained in account Assignment of Technical Object, Order is rejected to release since Settlement rule is not maintained.

Expectation is:

  • If the order type is linked to a Cost Center, the system will prefer the Cost Center as the receiver.
  • If the order type is linked to a WBS Element, the system will prefer the WBS Element as the receiver.

Am I missing anything here?

BR,

Riyaj

entry.title: Re: SAP Community Call for ALM – APJ edition
entry.content: Join our monthly webinar tailored for the APJ region to explore SAP's Application Lifecycle Management (ALM) offerings. Get the latest updates on SAP Cloud ALM, Solution Manager, Focused Build & Insights, and Focused Run, directly from SAP experts. Access slides and recordings here!

entry.title: TCODE IB52 serial number 000123 does not exist for material 123-3. Message no IS137
entry.content:
TCODE IB52 when I try to remove a serialized component from an Install Base the systems throws up the following message “Serial number 000000000000002422 does not exist for material 123-3. Message no. IS137”.

The serial number exists, I can view it in IQ02 and IQ09.

Process works for alpha-numeric serialized components.

Serial number 000000000000002422 does not exist for material 123-3

Message no. IS137

Diagnosis

The serial number that you have entered for the material does not exist in your system.

System Response

Processing will not be continued.

Procedure

Enter a valid serial number.

You can display the entries valid in your system by placing the cursor on the appropriate field and using the symbol for possible entries.

entry.title: How to define housekeeping settings or purchase additional DB storage in Cloud ALM
entry.content:
The Cloud ALM (CALM) DB consumption is steadily growing due to monitoring data collection that was activated.

How to retain all this data by purchasing additional DB storage or define a housekeeping approach and erase historical data?

Regards


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: How to define housekeeping settings or purchase addit...
entry.content:
Hello, 

1) To identify the Cloud ALM application that is using the most DB HANA size, please refer to the following KBA: ##3432729 - How to Monitor SAP Cloud ALM Memory Usage

2) After that, you can consult the following guide, which will help you adjust the housekeeping for each application: https://support.sap.com/en/alm/sap-cloud-alm/operations/expert-portal/housekeeping.html

3) If you require more memory, you have the option to subscribe to SAP Cloud ALM, tenant extension. This service provides an additional SAP Cloud ALM tenant with 8 GB of SAP HANA memory and 8 GB of monthly outbound API data transfer. For more information, please refer to the guide on Getting Additional SAP Cloud ALM Tenants.

Best regards

entry.title: Re: Enterprise Architecture for integration?
entry.content:
Team,

Now that SAP has acquired LeanIX and aligning its capabilities to realize the SAP EA Framework, the concept of creating/capturing Integration and API Artifacts is now available.  In my opinion these artefacts are cross domain - Business, Solution and Technical Architecture Domain.  I have also adopted ISAM - Integration Solution Advisory Methodology to support SAP EA Framework.

entry.title: formula for splitting fullname as firstname and lastname in BO 4.3
entry.content:
i was trying to split  a fullname with the below formula
as firstname , lastname (wherein fullname including with the middle name in it.)

Below Formula worked fine: 

First Name=Left(Substr([Name];Length(Substr([Name];1;Pos([Name];" "))+2);Length([Name]));Pos(Substr([Name];Length(Substr([Name];1;Pos([Name];" "))+2);Length([Name]));" "))

Last Name=Right(Substr([Name];Length(Substr([Name];1;Pos([Name];" "))+2);Length([Name]));Pos(Substr([Name];Length(Substr([Name];1;Pos([Name];" "))+2);Length([Name]));" ")+2)

QUESTION : Is there anyother simpler way we can achieve this and also Mid funtion is not available in Webi?

LAST_NAME: Mid(PATRN_NAME,1,InStr(PATRN_NAME,',')-1)

entry.title: Distribution rule data cannot reflect to G/L account report
entry.content:
In SAP Business ByDesign, after setting and running the overhead distribution rule for August and September in a testing environment, the results did not reflect in the G/L Item report. Guidance is needed on whether the "Total Costs by Cost Center and Assigned Projects" report can be used to verify the distribution.


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Distribution rule data cannot reflect to G/L account...
entry.content:
Dear Customer,

In SAP Business ByDesign, if you want to verify the results of your overhead distribution, you can use several reports to check if the overheads have been distributed and posted correctly. Here are a couple of reports you might find useful:

  • Total Costs by Cost Center and Assigned Projects:
    Yes, this report is a good starting point. It provides a summary of total costs incurred by cost centers and projects, including allocations. Check if the overhead amounts have been distributed correctly to the assigned cost centers or projects.

  • Cost Center - Line Items:
    This report provides detailed transactional data for each cost center. Verify if the overhead amounts are posted to the respective cost centers in this report.

  • Allocation Run Log:
    Navigate to the Overhead Run log. Check if the system successfully processed the allocation run and if there are any errors or unprocessed items.

  • Project Cost and Revenue Overview:
    If overhead is distributed to projects, this report can help confirm the amounts allocated to specific projects.

When reviewing these reports, make sure you: - Check the correct posting periods (for August and September in your case). - Verify the overhead accounts and cost elements. - Look for any error messages or logs from the overhead allocation run for additional clues. If you continue to have issues, it might be helpful to revisit the configuration of your overhead distribution rules to ensure everything is set up correctly. This might involve checking the allocation bases, percentages, and validity periods of the rules.

Also, posting of the Overhead distribution rule will have the journal entry type : 00073 - Distribution. So, can you try using the report G/L line item and filter it for the journal entry type.

entry.title: How can I assign operation from one resource to two or three resources using the “SAP_MULT_SCH”?
entry.content:
Hello, I have a question.
Thanks for all your support.

I am currently looking for a way to assign some operations on one resource to 2-3 resources using the “heuristic: SAP_MULT_SCH”.

If I use this heuristic to allocate operations, can you please tell me how to allocate operations equally or how to give priority to resource A (if resource A is overloaded, allocate to resource B).
Also, can other heuristics satisfy this requirement?

Thank you.

entry.title: Re: Launchpad Support Tools /UI2/FLPCM
entry.content: I just want to know if the tcode has been removed by SAP. Because I couldn't not find any documentation on that. That it splits into two tcodes.

entry.title: Backorders management
entry.content:
Hi All,

I need some help to understand hoe to manage backorders in system. For example.there are 500 open orders for dec. and by end of december we were able to complete only 450 orders. the remaining 50 orders adds up to january. How can I manage these 50 orders to confirm if these are true orders, or cancel them.

Any suggestions, to understand this process is appreciated, thanks

entry.title: Manufacturing Type ( Discrete and Process )
entry.content:
Hello,I know that production manufacturing methods include discrete manufacturing and process manufacturing and so on.

Then what's the difference between discrete manufacturing and process manufacturing ? More precisely, why would companies that seem better suited to process manufacturing adopt a discrete manufacturing?

To tell you in advance, I know the concept of most production methods. because I've seen a lot of related tables.

So, Please stop saying that the finished product can be broken down into raw materials or uses process orders or production orders as work instructions, and that you use Recipe and Formulas instead of BOM. Please don't say anything that seems to be on the website table.

My question is why would jam, laundry detergent, and bread-making companies choose Production Order instead of Process Manufacturing. Does bread and jam break down into flour again? From what I've seen on the internet, No. It can't be done.
But why are they making bread with the Production Order? Let me know if you know why they made that choice.

In my prediction, it's the CEO's mind, so we can't know everything.
But even though Process Manufacturing is a more suitable product(jam or bread), I wonder what difference it makes to choose Discrete Manufacturing.

Thank you for taking the time to think about it.

entry.title: Re: use of ST05 how can we trace a program in it
entry.content: Thanks mapla nallve purincu.

entry.title: How to keep the same cost in Sales order and return order

entry.title: Couldn't subscribe to Integration Suite.
entry.content:
please help me on the below error message while subscribing to integration suite

 

Couldn't subscribe to Integration Suite. Try again. If the problem persists, please post a question to SAP Community.

entry.title: Request License SAP Software Appliance (S/4Hana Onpremise)
entry.content:
Hi SAP Support,
Can we request permanent license for SAP Software Appliance S/4Hana (On-Premise?

Thanks
Trilo


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Request License SAP Software Appliance (S/4Hana Onpre...
entry.content:
Dear customer,

To generate license keys for SAP S/4HANA on-premise edition, follow the how-to steps below:

  1. Go to SAP for Me > License Keys
  2. Select/search for the installation number to use 
  3. Click + Add System
  4. Product: SAP S/4HANA 
  5. Version: Select from drop-down list
    • SAP S/4HANA 2023
    • SAP S/4HANA 2022
    • SAP S/4HANA 2021
    • SAP S/4HANA 2020
    • SAP S/4HANA 1909
    • SAP S/4HANA 1809
    • SAP S/4HANA 1709
    • SAP S/4HANA 1610
    • SAP S/4HANA On-Premise Edition 1511
  6. System ID: Enter any 3-character system ID
  7. System Name: Optional
  8. System Type: Select from drop-down list
  9. Database: Select from drop-down list
  10. Operating System: Select from drop-down list
  11. Click Continue
  12. Click + Add License Key
  13. License Type: Select from drop-down list
  14. Hardware Key: Enter hardware key found in SLICENSE
  15. Valid until: 31-12-9999
  16. Click Add
  17. Select license key from the list
  18. Click Generate (at the bottom)
  19. Install S/4HANA license key in SLICENSE

To obtain further information on how to request a license key for SAP S/4HANA, kindly refer to KBA 2366280 - How to request a license key for SAP S/4HANA (all versions) - SAP for Me

entry.content:

Is it like end users can create stories from scratch or they can copy the standard ones and modify it.

Thanks,

entry.title: Re: Error when create Actions in Process Automation with...
entry.content:
Hi 

->open the developer tools(network tab) while you use the destination to create the action project and look at response of "artifactInfo" API

->you need to give the url as your service url as SBPA appends /$metadata to it to get the metadata 

->also SBPA supports open api spec and EDMX format for metadata

hope it helps

entry.title: Re: How to get Values in Fiori Template Material BOM
entry.content:
1. You can maintain the different Production version (C223) to choose different  BOM according to the lot size.

For Example:

Production version: 0001    - >  BOM Alternative BOM: 01    Lot size: 0-100

Production version: 0002    - >  BOM Alternative BOM: 02    Lot size: 101-200

Production version: 0003    - >  BOM Alternative BOM: 03    Lot size: 201-99999999

2. If you want to mass change BOM, there is no standard app to upload a file to change the BOM, you should contact your developer to create a new program.

But you can use "Bill of Material Mass Maintenance" to mass change the BOM.

Links: SAP Fiori Apps Reference Library

entry.title: Re: Maximum Limit on Rebate | Settlement Management | Con...
entry.content:
Thanks a lot for your response. I have an additional question on the first one. If my rebate condition is a quantity condition and i add the maximum value as fixed amount, the condition exclusion doesnt work. Can you help me with what i may have been missed here.

Example: Rebate Condition is 20$ per EA, Maximum Value Condition is Fixed Amount , so 200$. The quanity is 115 EA.

Now i want the maximum condition 200$ to be applied instead of 20$/ EA. I have used the value of F in the condition exclusion procedure.

entry.title: Re: CAP Version 8 Update: Issue with $batch Function Call Responses
entry.content:
Hi @kevin_balboni1 ,

Nice catch.

I'm also able to reproduce the issue.
It is strange that the behaviour could be reproduced with earlier versions of 8, too (like 8.1.1).
The last working version was 7.9.5.

After some debugging I let it go.
First I thought that it is a problem with the batch itself (\node_modules\@sap\cds\libx\odata\middleware\batch.js) but replacing the whole file with the one from version 7.9.5 still doesn't seem to resolve the issue.

entry.title: Re: How a Citizen Developer Can Use SAP Build Process Automation to Automate Workshop Requests
entry.content:
Hi @AlexDong, this is a great example.

Does the BPA workflow support the "save as draft" functionality? Thank you

entry.title: Re: MDK Code Not Working on Android Device - Unable to Re...
entry.content: Instead of doing this in onLoad, I would suggest you use a rule for the Target of your data table that returns the result of your API call. If needed, you can still cache the result in Client Data and your rule can check first if cached data exists and return it or make the API call as needed.

entry.title: Re: Hi Please help me with error. G/L account 4000 cannot...
entry.content: Thanks for the Help

entry.title: Issue with AppUpdate.action Causing App Crash
entry.content:
Hello Everyone,

I have developed an MDK app and encountered an issue with the update functionality. Specifically, I have implemented an update button within the profile page of the app. When a user clicks this button, it initiates the update check and displays the available update. However, upon clicking the "Update Now" button, the app crashes in both the SAP Mobile Service client and the branded app (Android platform) I created.

For the update process, I am using the AppUpdate.action. Below are the steps I followed:

  1. User clicks the update button.
  2. The app checks for updates and displays the available update.
  3. User clicks "Update Now".
  4. The app crashes immediately.

Could you please assist me in resolving this issue? Any guidance or suggestions would be greatly appreciated.

Thank you for your support.

Hi @David_Chaviano,

Thank you.
Email(Gmail) from luluchu0516

entry.title: Re: MDK Code Not Working on Android Device - Unable to Re...
entry.content: Hi @bill_froelich, Thank you for the suggestion. Could you please provide an example of a rule for using the target of a data table? Additionally, which target should I choose: string target or function call target? Thanks for your help!

entry.title: Re: Issue with AppUpdate.action Causing App Crash
entry.content: You should review the logs on the client.  Most likely the logs will provide information about why the App Update bundle is causing a crash.  Do your metadata definitions try to reference an extension?

entry.title: Re: MDK Code Not Working on Android Device - Unable to Re...
entry.content: You would use a String Target pointed to your rule that returns the array of data to display.

entry.title: "DAOException: HibernateException" error in Onboarding
entry.content:
Hi All,

I am facing an error in ONB2.0 while updating the employee recurring pay componenent.

"java.lang.IllegalStateException: java.lang.IllegalStateException: com.successfactors.db.dao.DAOException: HibernateException on updating instance of type class com.successfactors.hris.bean.eo.EmpPayCompRecurringEO"

Can someone help me in resolving the issue?

Thanks, and Regards

Avinesh Sundar

entry.title: PDI BAdI to pass values to the additional fields of the XML in the SAFT report
entry.content:
In this blog, you learn how to use PDI-BAdI to customize the logic and pass data to the additional XML fields for SAFT reporting in SAP Business ByDesign.

In general, you perform the following to pass data to the XML fields:

  1.  Create the structure for additional sections and fields, which are then added to the main SAFT report/XML structure.
  2. Write the code to fetch data from the required Business Objects and develop the logic to pass data to the header section of XML fields.

The PDI-BAdI feature helps you avoid writing source code to fetch data from different Business Objects (BO) and assign values to additional section fields in the main source code of the SAFT report. You can use PDI-BAdI to write your own logic and pass data to the XML fields of the SAF-T report. You need to implement the "PDI-BAdI" in Cloud Application Studio to write logic that passes the data effectively.

The following steps guide you on how to implement the BAdI.

1. Create a structure and fields for additional sections of the SAF-T report's XML

Master, Source Documents and General Ledger Entries are the new additional sections added to the SAF-T Report’s XML Structure.

Master section contains General ledger Accounts, Customer , Supplier, Tax Table entry , Analysis Type table entry, Product, Asset, Taxonomies, UOM table, Movement Type Table, Physical stock and Owner structure and fields.

2. Open the Cloud Application studio and create a new solution. Enter the required parameter details.

Select the created solution and add a New Item

Select Enhancement Implementation and click on Add Button.

Enter the Namespace and Enhancement Option details

Enhancement Implementation is created

Partner can write their own logic to pass data to the XML fields of SAF-T report in the GENERATE_TAX_AUDIT_FILE.

Sample code logic to pass the values.

3. Create a new SAF-T report.

Go to work center General Ledger - SAF-T Report. Click on New -->Create New SAF-T report. Enter the required data in the selection parameters and click on Generate Button

New SAF-T report is created with status 'Finished'.

Select the Generated SAF-T Report and click on View results. Navigate to the Attachments tab.

Open the file and check newly added additions sections such as Master , Source documents and General Ledger entries present in it.

entry.title: Re: SAP Cloud Analytics Average Calculation
entry.content:
Hi @Abraham3,

Aggregation operates at the parent level, which is why it displays the same value as profit. To calculate the average, we need to consider child-level members. As shown in Screenshot 1, we first calculate the total profit, and in Screenshot 2, we determine the count of regions.

 

The chart illustrates the average profit region-wise.

 

Let me know if you'd like further refinements

Please upvote if the answer is correct!

Thanks.

entry.title: Re: Commerce Automation PO and GR from Non SAP to Ariba N...
entry.content:
Hi, you can send messages.

Mind that there is no concept of reversal in Ariba in a way you do it in SAP.

What Ariba will accept is negative receiving which becomes sort of "un-receiving" in Ariba Invoice Mgmt.

entry.title: Re: Troubleshooting SAP Certification discount code for special programs
entry.content:
HI ALL, 
IF YOU ARE FACING THIS ISSUE
"We are unable to provide invoice as a payment option at this point. Please contact SAP Accounts Receivable." 


SOLUTION THAT WORKS FOR ME BECAUSE I WAS APPLYING FROM MIDDLE EAST, I PUT 00000 as A ZIP CODE.
AND THEN CONTINUE OPTION IS SHOWING

IF IT WORKS GIVE A KUDO 😉

sorry but I prominent this message because this is the last week of code redemption.

entry.title: Updating PurchaseDownPayments with a service layer error: "Unallowed item cost [DPO1.INMPrice]
entry.content:
hello：

I encountered an error while updating my PurchaseDownPayments using the service layer, prompting "Unallowed item cost [DPO1. INMPrice][line: 1]"

My steps are as follows:

1. A PurchaseDownPayments was added, POST :https://IP:50000/b1s/v1/PurchaseDownPayments,code:

{
   "DocDate":"2024-12-24",
    "U_HBYID":"YFK2412240004",
    "DocumentLines":[
        {
            "InventoryQuantity":20,
            "TaxTotal":28.99,
            "ItemCode":"111020031",
            "VatGroup":"J2",
            "LineNum":1,
            "BaseType":-1,
            "Price":11.150442,
            "PriceAfterVAT":12.6,
            "LineTotal":223.01,
            "GrossTotal":252,
            "LastBuyInmPrice": 0.0,
            "StockInmPrice": 0.0,
            "UnitsOfMeasurment":1
        },
        {
            "InventoryQuantity":30,
            "TaxTotal":34.51,
            "ItemCode":"111020031",
            "VatGroup":"J2",
            "LineNum":2,
            "BaseType":-1,
            "Price":8.849558,
            "PriceAfterVAT":10,
            "LineTotal":265.49,
            "GrossTotal":300,
            "LastBuyInmPrice": 0.0,
            "StockInmPrice": 0.0,
            "UnitsOfMeasurment":1
        }
    ],
    "TaxDate":"2024-12-24",
    "Comments":"",
    "BPL_IDAssignedToInvoice":1,
    "DownPaymentType":"dptRequest",
    "CardCode":"S0000006",
    "DocDueDate":"2024-12-24",
    "DocTotal":552
}

 2. I changed the ratio to 80% in the client

3. I used the service layer for patch update, PATCH :https://IP:50000/b1s/v1/PurchaseDownPayments,code:

{
   "DocDate":"2024-12-24",
    "U_HBYID":"YFK2412240004",
    "DocumentLines":[
        {
            "InventoryQuantity":20,
            "TaxTotal":28.99,
            "ItemCode":"111020031",
            "VatGroup":"J2",
            "LineNum":1,
            "BaseType":-1,
            "Price":11.150442,
            "PriceAfterVAT":12.6,
            "LineTotal":223.01,
            "GrossTotal":252,
            "LastBuyInmPrice": 0.0,
            "StockInmPrice": 0.0,
            "UnitsOfMeasurment":1
        },
        {
            "InventoryQuantity":30,
            "TaxTotal":34.51,
            "ItemCode":"111020031",
            "VatGroup":"J2",
            "LineNum":2,
            "BaseType":-1,
            "Price":8.849558,
            "PriceAfterVAT":10,
            "LineTotal":265.49,
            "GrossTotal":300,
            "LastBuyInmPrice": 0.0,
            "StockInmPrice": 0.0,
            "UnitsOfMeasurment":1
        }
    ],
    "TaxDate":"2024-12-24",
    "Comments":"",
    "BPL_IDAssignedToInvoice":1,
    "DownPaymentType":"dptRequest",
    "CardCode":"S0000006",
    "DocDueDate":"2024-12-24",
    "DocTotal":552
}

 

Then I got an error and the version I was using was 2411.

The above operations I can directly operate in the client successfully.

entry.title: IT582 Exemption
entry.content:
Hello Experts,

As I am a freshman, guide me to achieve this requirement Internet allowance exemptions WT 2S31 (copied from MCAX). subtype for Exemptions infotype (0582) - "ZINT."

Under New Tax Reg paying through IT0014, need exemption until Rs. 5000.If the bill amount in IT582 exceeds the amount in IT0014, the full amount should be taxable. How can I achieve this?. Where can I see the changes in the RT log?

I have maintained below-listed tables & Schema

V_T7INT7: Define Tax Codes
V_T7INT9: Assign Tax Code Limits to Wage Types
V_T7INA9: Calculate Eligibility for RAPs
In schema (INN1) payroll function (INCTX),


 
My question is, where can I see those exemptions and taxable amounts?

If I check on PC 30 32 33, I am facing this error (screenshot below). 

By analysing the error, I don't have any duplicate entries in IT0014.

Also, suggest me PC, CC, and EC for the WT 2S31 and share a screenshot of the RT log /130 and /4.. series screenshot before and after exemptions.

Your input is appreciated. Thanks in advance.

entry.title: Re: The settlement rule receiver : Issue with S/4 HANA EA...
entry.content:
Greetings,

if you want to pull a settlement receiver from you technical object you need to maintain the default object type setting.

If your requirement is to have one default settlement object type per order type then this can be achieved by standard. If you order type can have a mix of default settlement object type (depending on what's maintained on reference technical object master data) then you might need to explore some user exits which would be triggered at settlement rule creation on specific order.

Best regards, M.

entry.title: Re: DataAction copy step

Hi @Bernardo_Santos1

Is the source data on the separated below 2 versions? 

  • Forecast: Forecast for the first six months of 2024
  • Prediction: Followed by predictions for the remaining months of the year.

In addition, how can we set the first six months? Does the end user dynamically set it using the External parameter(ex, 202406) or does Data action refer to the Version dimension property(ex, CurrentPeriod = 202406)? 

Moonjun

entry.title: Re-initializing of deltas in S4HANA from BW4HANA (For error Subscription for DTP* deleted in source)
entry.content:
Overview:

Re-initializing deltas between S4HANA and BW4HANA is vital for maintaining precise data synchronization. This process entails removing the existing initialization request in BW4HANA, purging previous data from the data targets, and establishing a new initialization. The error message indicates that the subscription for the Data Transfer Process (DTP) has been removed in the source system (S4HANA).

To address this issue, examine the DTP settings in BW4HANA, recreate the subscription if necessary, review system logs for further details, and re-initialize the delta process.

Error Occurred in BW4HANA System:

Subscription for DTP* deleted in source system. The error is due to deletion on subscription in S4 side or the subscription of DTP is mapped to wrong source system.

 

Check ODQMON by putting data source name in queue and see the subscriptions. 

 

Solution to the error:

Step 1) To delete a subscription in T-Code ODQMON in S4HANA, open ODQMON, select the DataSource, go to the "Subscriptions" tab, and delete the desired subscription. Confirm the deletion by checking the "Queues" tab to ensure the request is inactive. 

 

Step 2) To initiate a new subscription from BW4HANA to S4HANA, it is essential to delete all existing requests in the target system. If these requests are not removed, you will encounter errors. Therefore, ensure that all pending requests are cleared before proceeding with the new subscription setup. This step is crucial to avoid any interruptions or issues during the process.

Open the monitor in SAP GUI

Change overall Status to RED 

And delete the requests one by one. Please ensure all the requests are deleted from the target before moving to next step.

Step 3) To begin, manually initiate the Data Transfer Process (DTP) in delta mode using Eclipse. After starting the DTP, check the ODQMON transaction in your S4HANA system to confirm that a new subscription has been created. This verification step is crucial to ensure that the data transfer setup is functioning correctly.

 

Note** If you continue to encounter errors on the BW4HANA side after completing the previous steps, please run the report RODPS_REPL_TEST in transaction SE38 for the data source on the S4HANA side. This report will help diagnose and resolve any remaining issues with the data transfer process.

entry.title: Re: too many tags

The new feature has been in process and might be released early 2025. Kindly wait further. Best regards, SAP Support

entry.title: Re: Question on COF
entry.content:
Dear PCA, 

1. If you have added JA in the COF, it should be in the export of the COF. Please try again, and if you see the same symptom, please make it sure to be shared with the SAP Delivery Team in S2DHO meeting verbally. (You should highlight that JA language is required - to the PL or PC3 team in the S2DHO meeting)

2. Please finalise the solution range before closing the COF. Once closed, it cannot be edited. Also, please select S/4HANA EM if you are referring to the core RISE system. 

Hope this helped! 

Regards, Rin

entry.title: Re: Can user has user defined header/item condition in Sa...

entry.title: Re: Supplier Enablement Manage Vendors

I have got the answer to the question posted. We need to enable a feature and an entitlement with the help of SAP Ariba Support. Please check the KBA https://support.ariba.com/item/view/KB0401894 for more details on how to get the manage vendors option under Supplier enablement. After SAP Ariba Support team enabled the settings, am able to see the Manage Vendors option under Supplier Enablement.

Thanks,
Velu

entry.title: Re: Registration for Indirect Taxation Abroad (RITA) in 2302 - A Pragmatic Approach

Hope you all are doing well!

Does it mean that even after Country activation, we will not be able to create "IE" as a foreign plant for using the RITA functionality of Plant Abroad in both these Company Codes?

Please guide.

 

No.Country/Region RITA enabledForeign plant enabledCountry/Region activation required
   (Y/N)(Y/N)*(Y/N)**
11IEIrelandYNY

entry.title: Enhancing ABAP Code Security During Migration from ECC to S/4HANA
entry.content:
Migrating from SAP ECC to S/4HANA isn’t just a technical upgrade—it’s a complete reboot of your system. Developing and refining ABAP code becomes a key part of the process, as the entire logic of your business processes relies on it. But alongside the updated functionality, new potential vulnerabilities often emerge that could compromise security and even derail the successful launch of S/4HANA.

If you’re planning or already in the middle of migration, don’t overlook code security. In new scenarios—whether integrating with external services, updating custom modules, or building unique logic—one missed vulnerability can expose your sensitive data to significant risk.

 

Where the Risks Lie

  • New and adapted programs. When transferring legacy Z-programs or writing new ABAP code, it’s easy to miss SQL injections, backdoors, or other vulnerabilities.
  • Third-party components. If you’re implementing add-ons developed by external vendors, their code must also be thoroughly reviewed.
  • Integrations and extensions. Connecting to external services or integrating with SAP BTP increases the likelihood of potential security gaps.

During large-scale migrations to S/4HANA, there often isn’t enough time or resources to manually review every piece of code—especially when your developers are already knee-deep in deadlines.

How RedRays Can Help

RedRays has developed an AI-powered solution specifically designed to identify vulnerabilities in ABAP code. Its standout feature is the so-called “AIR mode,” where code analysis runs entirely in-memory.

What does this mean?

  • No traces. The scanner doesn’t store logs or export your source code, so your data remains completely confidential.
  • Real-time analysis. Results are available almost instantly, allowing developers to fix critical issues immediately.

Types of Vulnerabilities It Detects

  • Open SQL / Native SQL Injection
  • Directory Traversal
  • ABAP Command Injection
  • Operating System Command Injection
  • Unauthorized entry points and access
  • Various backdoors and logic errors

With RedRays, you won’t miss critical vulnerabilities—its AI excels at identifying patterns and risks that might seem harmless at first glance.

A Simple Start: Integration and Workflow

Seamless Integration with Your Tools

RedRays easily integrates with the IDEs you already use, including:

  • Eclipse (commonly used for ABAP development)
  • VS Code (a versatile, cross-platform IDE)
  • SAP Business Application Studio (SAP BTP’s native environment)

Once the plugin is installed, simply configure the URL and API key, and you’re ready to start scanning your code.

For Developers

  • Instant Feedback. After scanning, you’ll immediately receive a list of vulnerabilities along with recommendations for resolving them.
  • Prioritization. Issues are categorized by severity (Critical, High, Medium, Low), so you know what to address first.
  • User-Friendly Interface. RedRays provides step-by-step guidance for fixing each identified issue.

By embedding security checks directly into your daily workflow, you can proactively eliminate risks without waiting for something to break in production.

Why This Is Crucial During Migration to S/4HANA

Migrating to S/4HANA often brings significant changes to architecture and business processes. This means:

  • Data breach risks. Any vulnerabilities in new modules can lead to unauthorized access to sensitive information.
  • Compliance issues. Regulatory requirements like GDPR, SOX, and others can result in hefty fines if your system is not secure.
  • Reputation damage. Failures and security incidents can undermine trust with clients, partners, and investors.

Code security is often one of those aspects remembered “last minute,” but it’s the foundation of stable and secure operations in your S/4HANA environment.

How to Get Started with RedRays

  1. Install the plugin for your IDE (Eclipse, VS Code, or SAP Business Application Studio).
  2. Set up the URL and API key to activate the scanner.
  3. Run a scan. Select the code you need to analyze and get a detailed report in just one click.
  4. Fix vulnerabilities. Follow the recommendations provided by RedRays to patch issues before deployment.

Conclusion

Migrating to S/4HANA is not just about new functionality and optimization—it’s also about adopting updated security practices. The earlier you begin analyzing and improving your ABAP code, the fewer unpleasant surprises you’ll encounter down the line.

RedRays makes this process faster and easier. Its “AIR mode” ensures your code remains private, while intelligent algorithms detect even the trickiest vulnerabilities.

If you want to make your S/4HANA migration safe and stress-free, visit https://redrays.io/abap-scanner/ to learn more and get started.

entry.title: Re: Kit to stock process trigger from EWM using VAS order...
entry.content:
Hi

I am sharing it but you need to understand this carefully

Symptom

The error code L9029 can have more than one symptom when EWM queues are involved:

  • It might occur after adding a packing item for a Return to Vendor Delivery in the EWM system. The qRFC to update the ECC system gets stuck with the error code L9029 - "Error while determining ref.mov.type for WM via Table 156S". In the long text of the error message, you see that movement type 161 is mentioned;
  • In a kitting reversal process, after triggering the creation of an inbound delivery from EWM, the /SPE/INB_DELIVERY_SAVEREPLICA message gets stuck in ERP with error "Error while determining ref.mov.type for WM via Table 156S: 101/X/X/X/X/X/";
  • The error L9029 is called during the replication of a direct outbound delivery from EWM to the ERP system;
  • The problem occurs while trying to add a delivery item to an existing delivery, usually done via the replication of a BAPI_OUTB_DELIVERY_CHANGE queue.

Environment

  • SAP ERP Central Component (ECC)
  • SAP Extended Warehouse Management (EWM)
  • SAP S/4HANA on premise 

Cause

Incorrect customizing.

Resolution

  1. The movement type 161 is not appropriate for packaging materials as it is only foreseen to be used for items with reference to a purchase order. For such cases, it is necessary to change the configuration so that, for example, movement type 601 is used instead. This is also described in the SAP Help Portal.

Additional packaging material items are supported in the following outbound deliveries:

  • Outbound deliveries with reference to a sales order
  • Replenishment deliveries with reference to a stock transport order
  • Direct outbound deliveries for the customer
  • Direct outbound deliveries to another plant

  1. The error message indicates that the wrong delivery type with wrong movement type 101 was chosen. Note that this scenario is not a normal inbound delivery scenario (type 'EL') with reference to a purchase order. Delivery type/item category must be 'HID'/'HIDN' or 'DIG'/'DIGN' respectively, defined in table /SPE/DIRDLV_TYPE. The Business Functions LOG_LE_INTEGRATION and LOG_PP_EWM_MAN also have to be activated to make this process functional. Note 1423321 explains about these Business Functions. For inbound deliveries, movement type 501 or 521 has to be assigned to the item category in 0VLP transaction, while for outbound deliveries the correct movement type is 601.

  2. In the customizing settings of transaction 0VLK, ensure that the field "Order required" is filled with value 'Goods movement through inb.deliv. / post.chge /outb. deliv.'. Test the process again after the change.

  3. Check the IMG activity Logistics Execution -> Extended Warehouse Management Integration -> Outbound Process -> Direct Outbound Deliveries -> Determine Item Categories for Direct Outbound Deliveries. As explained in this IMG Help:

"If you add items to an existing outbound delivery in EWM the system does not use the parameters "Code for the Initiator of a Communication Chain" and "EWM Delivery Type" for the item category determination; the item category determination is called with value SPACE for these parameters."

Hence, review the relevant entry in this customizing considering the item category and movement type used.

 

See if it works 

Regards

Rashid

entry.title: Re: Program to delete already released transports
entry.content: I developed custom program using FM TRINT_DELETE_COMM

entry.title: Download PDF locally (SAP R3, 4.0B)
entry.content:
Hello experts,
The client I am working on has SAP R3 version 4.0B! The requirement is to download a PDF file locally. The file is stored in the application server. Function module GUI_DOWNLOAD is not available in ABAP thus I am using WS_DOWNLOAD. I use open dataset for output in binary mode but then I don't know how to use 

READ DATASET since there is no xstring-equivalent to read into. Has anyone got any idea how to approach this?

Thank you in advance,

Konstantinos

entry.title: Re: Error HUGENERAL066 in LI20 During Inventory Write-Off
entry.content:
see this thread, it has same error in different scenario it may help you to solve

https://community.sap.com/t5/enterprise-resource-planning-q-a/transfer-stock-between-hu-with-different-inspection-lot/qaq-p/8860043

entry.title: Re: formula for splitting fullname as firstname and lastn...
entry.content:
Define the following variables:

v_FirstName:    =Left([fullname];Pos([fullname]; " "))

v_LastName:  =Right([fullname];Length([fullname])-Rpos([fullname]; " "))

 

...

Hope it helps

entry.title: Re: FM_DLOI Error
entry.content:
Hi priyadarshan_behera,

I was stuck in the same situation, the step i was missing is to execute FM_DLOI  multiple times for the PO, without Do not delete documents in FYC marked. 

Please also make sure Period 16 is open for the FY affected in FMIR for the relevant value type.

Thanks

Atif

entry.title: Re: Migrate your data: FI - Accounts payable open item
entry.content: Here I attach a sample file for FI-Accounts payable open item, you can check and compare with your file to see if you can find the problem.

entry.title: ๊Update company info number for S-User
entry.content:
How can the super admin update company info number for S-User?


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: ๊Update company info number for S-User
entry.content:
Dear customer, 

Unfortunately, the Super Admin does not have the ability to update the phone number in the Company Info section. Only the S-user has the capability to do so.

To update the phone number in the Company Info section, kindly instruct the user to follow the steps provided below: 

1. Access the following link: https://accounts.sap.com/ui/protected/profilemanagement
2. Navigate to the "Personal Information" section and click on "Edit"
3. Enter the new telephone number and click "Verify"
4. A code will be sent to the new telephone number. Please enter the code in the "Code" section.
5. Finally, click "Submit"

Please let me know if you have any further questions or concerns. 

Best regards,
Judy

entry.title: Re: Migrate your data: FI - Accounts payable open item

entry.title: Re: SAP BusinessObjects BI 4.3 SP4 Patch 900 release note...
entry.content:
the "new release note" is correct now,
in my experience it takes almost 2-3 days after the patch is released until it is available (as PDF file)
I always used the "Content Info" to view the change list until the new release note (as PDF) is available.

entry.title: Re: Change employee group and sub group
entry.content: Even if you process the payroll, Thorough PA41 you can change the employee sub group.

entry.title: Re: Double slash (//) issue in Adobe forms
Re: Create a SQL user with access to multiple spaces in SAP Datasphere
entry.content:
@Sefan_Linders  Thank you!

"I have a doubt about the meaning of the following syntax:

SQL
 
SELECT OBJECT_NAME FROM effective_privileges;

When I tried this code, it returned tables/views from the SYS schema. It did not return the tables/views I expected from the specified space."

entry.title: Re: SAP Single Sign-on : Kerberos/SPNEGO Setup for AS-JAVA
entry.content: Is this blog suitable for SAP JAVA below 7.40 i.e 7.31
